steps.title: Identify what the blank refers to
steps.content: The previous sentence says: "Another example is 'rodeo.'" The blank begins the next sentence: "______ comes from the Spanish word *rodear*." The blank is a pronoun replacing "rodeo" — a word, not a person. The parallel sentence about "lasso" uses "It comes from the Spanish word lazo," confirming the pronoun needed is non-human.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and pick the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — It: A non-human pronoun that correctly refers back to the word "rodeo." Matches the parallel structure used for "lasso" in the previous example. ✓
  • Choice B — Someone: Refers to an unspecified person. No person is being discussed — the subject is the word "rodeo." ✗
  • Choice C — She: A feminine personal pronoun. No female person has been introduced as the subject of this sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — He: A masculine personal pronoun. No male person has been introduced as the subject of this sentence. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
